context: =  uncommon benign tumor composed of hematopoietic cells + mature fat resembling bone marrow Prevalence:   0.08-0.20% (autopsy series); 3-6% of all incidental primary adrenal tumors Mean age:   55 years (for adrenal myelolipoma); M÷F = 2÷3; 64 years (for extraadrenal myelolipoma) Cause:   chronic ACTH elevation → metaplasia of adrenocortical cells precipitated by chronic stress / degeneration Path:   mature fat (0-100%) interspersed with hematopoietic cells resembling bone marrow + pseudocapsule Histo:   variable mixture of megakaryocytes, myeloid and leukocyte precursor cells, erythroid cells
